message:
fix(e2e): unroute prior mockAPI handlers so latest call wins (Fixes #9085) (#9247)

The `mockAPI` fixture in `web/e2e/fixtures.ts` registered Playwright
`page.route()` handlers but never called `page.unroute()`. Playwright
stacks route handlers and matches them in registration order, so when a
`beforeEach` set up `mockClusters([A])` and the test body called
`mockClusters([B])`, the first handler kept winning and the test
silently received the stale `A` data.

This change:

- Tracks every URL pattern the fixture registers in a per-test Map.
- Unroutes any prior handlers for the same pattern before registering a
  new one, giving callers obvious "most recent call wins" semantics.
- Adds a public `unrouteAll()` helper for tests that want to start from
  a clean slate after `beforeEach` set up defaults.
- Adds fixture-teardown cleanup so handlers cannot leak across tests
  when Playwright reuses a page (serial mode, manual reuse).
- Centralizes URL patterns and the mock HTTP status as named constants
  (no magic numbers; keeps `setRoute()` and `unrouteAll()` in sync).

Same root cause as the comment at `Missions.spec.ts:85-91` referencing
PR #6474. No production code changes; e2e fixture only.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
